online_1.py.justification: Clear domain model with descriptive names and docstrings, but redundant generator wrapping, a confusing x/y vs row/col axis muddle, and a hidden-trees-then-subtract detour add unnecessary friction.

online_2.py.justification: Four near-identical direction methods, a magic `i = -100` sentinel, a brittle `square grid` assumption, and a confusing dual-purpose return tuple make this hard to follow despite type hints.

synth_clean.py.justification: Cleanly factored with a reusable `_ray` abstraction, consistent row/col naming, a module docstring, and tidy top-level functions; the most elegant and readable of the set.

synth_mid.py.justification: Compact and very readable with a shared `line_of_sight` generator and DIRECTIONS table eliminating duplication; loses a little only for the flat function-based design without a grid abstraction.

synth_primitive.py.justification: One giant `go` function with eight nearly-identical inlined direction loops, single-letter names throughout, and zero decomposition makes this tedious and error-prone to read.

tomek.py.justification: Well-decomposed with an Enum-driven direction model and clear function names, but the underused Forest wrapper, threaded forest argument everywhere, and an external read_input dependency add mild ceremony.
